methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed stage climate & energy company founded by Olivia Dippo that has raised $6M to date. The target operates within a sector encompassing 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach. Using the $6M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for pre-seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $15M. The peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow climate & energy peer set; while 21 same-sector peers exist, none of the targeted filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) yielded the minimum three datapoints required for robust triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, with a median of $15M. This range reflects the 2.5x multiplier methodology applied conservatively and represents approximately 2.2x the current capital base. The primary valuation uncertainty stems from the absence of direct peer benchmarking; reliance on a single progression method limits confidence in the range precision. Additional uncertainty derives from typical pre-seed stage risks including product-market fit validation and competitive positioning within the fragmented climate technology sector.
